text: Hi, my name is Scott Chaney. I am a urologist at the Mayo Clinic in Phoenix, Arizona.
Today we're going to be doing a case of a partial nephrectomy for a renal tumor in a forty-nine-year-old obese man with a five-centimeter posterior tumor.
The surgeon plans to free the kidney from surrounding tissues and rotate it to access the posterior side for performing a partial nephrectomy on the renal tumor.
I plan to do the case off clamp without clamping the vessels to the kidney, but I always have that option available for me if the bleeding gets intense. So we're going to see how this goes.
